adds.rationale: Pure-play SMR exposure to back the user's near-term fission thesis; NuScale is the only US developer with NRC design certification and the AI/data-center power-demand narrative is gathering steam in summer 2023 financial press.

news_evidence.summary: US NRC issues final rule certifying NuScale's 50 MWe SMR design, the first SMR design ever certified in the United States.
NuScale and UAMPS update Carbon Free Power Project cost target to ~$89/MWh, sparking debate over SMR economics but keeping the Idaho project alive through 2023.
Reports highlight surging data-center power demand from AI workloads, with utilities and analysts naming next-generation nuclear (including SMRs) as a structural beneficiary.

adds.rationale: Broaden the under-represented aging-population/healthcare wave beyond a single robotic-surgery name (ISRG); XLV is the cheapest, most liquid US large-cap healthcare basket and captures the GLP-1 obesity-drug breakout already visible by mid-2023.

news_evidence.summary: Novo Nordisk SELECT trial shows Wegovy cuts cardiovascular events 20%, re-rating the entire GLP-1 weight-loss category and lifting US large-cap pharma/healthcare sentiment.
US 65+ population projected to grow from 58M to 82M by 2040; CMS spending forecasts rising healthcare share of GDP through the decade.
Eli Lilly Mounjaro/tirzepatide sales beat; analysts upgrade large-cap pharma earnings outlook on diabetes/obesity franchise.

removes.rationale: AI/semis bucket is overstacked at 5 tickers (AAPL, MSFT, GOOGL, NVDA, SMH); NVDA already delivers concentrated upside on the dominant AI wave, so the broad semis basket adds correlated exposure without unique thesis lift.

news_evidence.summary: NVDA Q2 FY24 revenue $13.5B, up 101% YoY, data-center segment $10.3B; the AI capex cycle is concentrating in a handful of names rather than the broad semis basket.
SMH year-to-date performance is heavily driven by its NVDA weight (~10% of fund); ex-NVDA semis basket lagging, reinforcing the case for direct NVDA exposure over the ETF wrapper.

removes.rationale: Heavy overlap with URA on the nuclear thesis; URA gives tighter exposure to uranium miners (the cleaner buildup-phase play), and freeing this slot makes room for the SMR pure-play which advances the user's near-term fission thesis more directly.

news_evidence.summary: Uranium spot price breaks above $65/lb in September 2023, the highest since 2011, driven by supply constraints (Niger coup) and revived demand from new reactor builds.
NLR holdings analysis: ETF blends utility operators with uranium miners, diluting the pure miner/upstream exposure that URA delivers; performance YTD trails URA.

rationale_overall: Net effect: tighten the AI bucket (5 to 4) by dropping the redundant semis ETF, swap one of two overlapping nuclear ETFs for a pure-play SMR name to sharpen the near-term fission thesis, and add broad US healthcare exposure to give the under-represented aging-demographics wave a second leg. Watchlist stays at the cap of 16 with cleaner thematic shape: AI 4, nuclear 3 (URA, CCJ, SMR), rockets 1, robotics 1, quantum 1, energy/geopolitical 2, demographics 2, defense 1, general 1.
